understanding.goal_understanding: Place every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+' at the same time to trigger WIN for the level, and continue until the full run reports WIN (completing all levels within the step budget).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A single piece can be active/selected at a time. CLICKing on certain reachable/valid tiles teleports (or path-moves) the currently selected piece directly to that tile; if the destination is invalid (wall/obstacle/out of bounds/not a legal tile), the piece does not move and a step is still consumed. MOVE_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT attempts to move the selected piece by one tile; if blocked by a wall/obstacle or otherwise not permitted, the piece stays in place and still consumes a step. In my attempts, movement was frequently blocked, suggesting tight walls/obstacles and that only specific tiles were legal CLICK destinations.

orient_history: Movement is by 1 cell (3 pixels) per MOVE_* and blocked by collidable walls/selectables. CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled but does not move it through walls. Need to get a selectable onto every goal tile; however both selectables appear fully enclosed, so the key is probably selecting/activating a piece or interaction that changes walls via CLICK on specific tiles/objects.

Movement keys attempt to shift selected piece by its size (3 px = 1 cell) but are currently impossible due to surrounding wall enclosure. CLICK likely relocates a selectable directly to the clicked position (if valid), enabling placement onto goal tiles without pathing. To win, we must place a selectable onto the goal area; possibly only one goal region needs occupancy by any selectable.

CLICK moves the selected piece to a clicked location only if the destination area is not blocked by walls; otherwise no movement. Since sliding moves are fully blocked, progress requires CLICKing directly onto an open goal tile area. The goal at [35,17] is likely reachable by CLICK if not inside wall collision.

CLICK teleports/moves the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel position only if the destination area is empty (not inside walls/bounds). Movement actions attempt to shift by 3 pixels (1 cell) but are blocked by adjacent walls. We likely need to select the other piece (S) and move/teleport it onto the goal, since current selected piece is trapped and cannot reach the goal.

Clicks seem to teleport/move the selected piece to the clicked pixel position only if that destination is a valid empty space; MOVE_* cannot work while surrounded by walls. To reach the goal, we likely must use CLICK to place a selectable onto a goal tile directly (or into an open corridor if any). Need to select/move a piece onto any cell within the goal area.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): this is a sliding/step movement puzzle where MOVE_* attempts to translate the currently selected 3x3 piece by one cell (3 pixels), but it cannot move if any adjacent collision occurs. CLICK likely only selects/teleports between existing selectable instances, not to arbitrary tiles. Since all moves are currently blocked, we must change selection context or find a movable piece; but forced reframe requires a different action type now, so we will probe with a MOVE action even if blocked to confirm movement rules.

CLICK teleports the currently selected piece to the clicked cell if it’s a valid empty location; clicks that land on walls/invalid cells do nothing. To win, we need a selectable occupying the goal tiles; since movement is blocked, we should use CLICK to place a piece onto the goal region, likely selecting and placing each piece onto different goal cells if required.

Movement via MOVE_* is grid-stepped by 3 pixels (1 cell) but can be fully blocked when adjacent walls surround the 3x3 piece. CLICK seems to attempt to reposition the selected piece directly to the clicked cell if it’s a valid empty landing region; otherwise no effect. Since we are fully boxed in for MOVE_*, we must use CLICK to reposition to an open area nearer the goal.

Movement with MOVE_* shifts by 3 pixels (1 cell) but is currently impossible due to surrounding wall tiles. CLICK seems to attempt to move/teleport the selected piece to the clicked location if valid; blocked clicks do nothing. Likely need to CLICK a reachable empty/goal tile area to reposition, since walking is fully blocked.

Hypothesis: CLICK teleports/swaps the selected piece to a clicked reachable empty cell only if the destination is valid (not inside wall geometry and within some connected open region). Current selected is trapped; to make progress we must select the other selectable (S) via CLICK on its location, then maneuver/click it toward the goal region. WIN likely requires occupying the single goal area with at least one selectable (maybe exact overlap).

Hypothesis: This level uses CLICK-to-swap/teleport between existing selectable instances/anchors; movement keys only work when not enclosed. Since current selected is trapped, we must CLICK another reachable selectable/anchor to change which instance is selected (or swap positions), then continue positioning toward the goal.

New hypothesis: CLICK is not the primary movement; instead, the selected piece moves with MOVE_* but can only move when there is free space (not a wall). Since the currently selected piece is fully enclosed, progress requires switching selection to the other selectable (likely not enclosed) and then using MOVE_* to navigate toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* shifts by one cell (3 px) but only within open space; currently impossible due to surrounding walls. CLICK attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the target coordinate if it is a valid empty cell (not wall, within bounds), effectively a teleport. To win, we likely need to place one selectable onto the goal region (goal tile at pixel [35,17]) and possibly keep others elsewhere; only one goal object exists so only one piece must occupy it.

Hypothesis: CLICK teleports/moves the currently selected piece to the clicked location only if the destination is reachable/within same open region (not blocked by walls) and not occupied; MOVE shifts by 1 cell (3 px) but is blocked by walls. Currently P is trapped, so progress requires selecting and moving the other selectable S, which may have mobility toward the goal region.

CLICK on the other selectable’s current position swaps selection (or moves selected to that selectable), allowing us to control S which may have mobility toward the goal corridor. To make progress we should switch to the other selectable at [12,18] and then navigate toward the goal area.

Movement seems to be by 3-pixel (1 cell) increments with collision against walls. CLICK appears to attempt teleport/move selection to a target coordinate, but it only succeeds when the destination is a valid empty spot; clicks on/near walls or inside blocked regions do nothing. Since P is boxed in, progress likely requires switching control to the other selectable (S) via CLICK on it, then navigating/teleporting S to the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a reachable empty cell relocates the currently selected piece to that cell if the placement fits (no wall overlap). If CLICK is on another selectable, it swaps/teleports selection/position between the two fixed selectable anchors. Goal tile is non-collidable; to win, we need both selectables simultaneously covering the goal area (or at least occupying distinct goal cells). Since movement is blocked, we must use CLICK-based repositioning to place pieces onto goal cells.

New hypothesis: CLICK teleports/switches the selected piece between a small set of free pockets inside a mostly-walled map; the arrow MOVE actions only work when a piece is aligned with an opening, but right now the selected piece is fully enclosed so MOVE won’t change position. To make progress, we likely must change which piece is selected and then use MOVE to explore if that other piece has an available direction.

CLICK likely selects a selectable at the target position; if clicking another selectable, control switches to it (or swaps), effectively moving the active piece to that selectable’s location. Direct movement is impossible from current boxed cells, so the only progress is to switch selection to the other selectable and attempt different interactions from there (e.g., see if it can move or be switched onto the goal).

Hypothesis: MOVE_* attempts to shift selected piece by its size (3px=1 cell) but P is boxed in by walls. CLICK on another selectable swaps/teleports the selected piece to that selectable’s position (effectively selecting/moving between the two). CLICK on non-selectable tiles (including goal) has no effect if blocked/invalid. To win, we likely need to get a selectable onto the goal by clicking an intermediate valid target or selecting the other piece if it can reach goal.

CLICK acts like a teleport/move command only when the clicked location is a valid empty destination (likely reachable/inside same open room). The goal area appears separated by walls, so direct clicking into it fails. Since the currently selected piece at [18,21] is immobile, we likely must switch selection to the other selectable at [12,18] and maneuver it to the goal, potentially needing both pieces to occupy goal tiles if multiple goal cells count as separate goals (but here only one goal object).

Movement keys are blocked due to surrounding walls. CLICK seems to act like selecting/teleporting between available selectable pieces/positions, but does not move into walls or directly onto the distant goal. Hypothesis: CLICK moves the selected piece to the clicked position only if that destination is a valid anchor for the piece (not overlapping walls/out-of-bounds and likely within some reachable set). Movement keys only work when adjacent space is free, but both selectables are boxed in by walls so MOVE is useless here. CLICK likely selects/moves the currently selected piece to the clicked selectable's position (i.e., swap/teleport between fixed selectable anchors). To win, we likely need to click directly onto a goal tile to teleport the selected piece there (or a specific goal-adjacent anchor), since physical movement is impossible.

Direct movement is impossible due to walls. To win, we likely need to get BOTH selectables onto goal tiles, but we can’t move them; maybe one selectable can be placed onto the goal by clicking directly on a goal tile while a piece is selected (a placement action). To win, at least one selectable must be placed onto the goal region; likely requires selecting the other piece (at [18,21]) and then finding a valid CLICK destination on/near the goal if such teleport is allowed, or swapping selection state by clicking it first.

Movement by MOVE_* is impossible due to being enclosed by walls; progress must come from CLICK interactions. CLICK seems to move the selected piece to the clicked position only if that destination is a valid empty space (not wall/blocked) and likely aligned to the 3-pixel grid. To win, we likely need to place the selectable onto the goal region at pixel ~[35,17]; try clicking directly on a goal tile coordinate that is within the '+' region and aligned to multiples of 3.

Clicking on another selectable's current position swaps/teleports the selected piece to that selectable's spot. Clicking arbitrary empty/goal cells doesn't move if unreachable/blocked. Both selectables are trapped in wall pockets; the only effective action observed is swapping between the two selectables. With only one goal and no pathing, the likely win condition is to occupy the goal by selecting/clicking directly on the goal tile (or within it), but previous clicks near it failed—maybe we must click exactly the goal's top-left pixel position [35,17].

New hypothesis: CLICK swaps/teleports the selected piece between fixed selectable anchor positions, but movement (arrow actions) only works if not enclosed; currently P is immobile. To make any progress with 2 steps left, we must switch selection to the other selectable (S), hoping it is not enclosed and can move toward the goal with a MOVE action.

Clicking seems to teleport/move the currently selected piece only if the target tile is a valid reachable/allowed placement. The goal area appears unreachable for the currently selected piece due to a central wall mass; movement is impossible from current position. Only alternative is to select/move the other selectable (at [12,18]) with a final click, hoping it can be placed onto the goal directly (or at least change selection/placement to satisfy win if only one piece needed).

Hypothesis: Actions consume steps; with steps_remaining=0 the game will not execute further moves/clicks, so the level cannot be completed from this state. Need a reset/retry mechanic, but none is available in the allowed action set.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Place every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+' at the same time to trigger WIN for the level, and continue until the full run reports WIN (completing all levels within the step budget).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: A single piece can be active/selected at a time. CLICKing on certain reachable/valid tiles teleports (or path-moves) the currently selected piece directly to that tile; if the destination is invalid (wall/obstacle/out of bounds/not a legal tile), the piece does not move and a step is still consumed. MOVE_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT attempts to move the selected piece by one tile; if blocked by a wall/obstacle or otherwise not permitted, the piece stays in place and still consumes a step. In my attempts, movement was frequently blocked, suggesting tight walls/obstacles and that only specific tiles were legal CLICK destinations.
